stt.Label: Speech Recognition
stt.Title: Speech-to-Text

DetailedTable.Title: Detailed Price Breakdown
DetailedTable.SubTitle: Complete pricing for each service with detailed billing unit descriptions

Q1.Question: What are tokens and how are they counted?
Q1.Answer: A token is a unit of text, roughly 3–4 characters or about ¾ of a word in English. For voice dialogues, tokens from both the recognized speech (input) and the generated response (output) are counted. 1 million tokens ≈ 750,000 words.

Q2.Question: What's the difference between Realtime AI and Text AI?
Q2.Answer: Realtime AI handles voice calls — including speech recognition (STT), LLM response generation, and text-to-speech synthesis (TTS). Text AI works with text in chats and widgets only, which is why it's more affordable.

Q3.Question: How is Speech Analytics billed?
Q3.Answer: First, the recording is transcribed (billed under STT — per minute). Then the resulting transcription is analyzed via LLM (billed under Analytics — per token). These are two independent steps with different pricing.

Q4.Question: Is there a minimum payment or subscription?
Q4.Answer: No. You pay only for actual usage. There are no monthly fees, minimum packages, or mandatory subscriptions. Top up your balance and use services as needed.
